title: eriolepis ferox
side_effects: Eriolepis ferox can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach ulcers due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, rashes, and even blisters. Ingestion of large quantities can lead to cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and even cardiac arrest. Improper handling of the plant can also cause allergic reactions, including respiratory distress, in some individuals.
